how: Moved bounds selection, fallback cap, and batch marker creation into an owner-local helper.
problem: The Worker API returned data, but the front end iterated raw KTO candidates in one effect and created map markers too aggressively.
remainingGap: Graphify source graph freshness remained a governance follow-up.
why: Prevent mobile renderer stalls from unbounded KTO marker creation.

how: Added NaverMarkerConfig materialization limits and center-distance cap selection.
problem: Normal Naver map bounds could still allow too many KTO candidates to materialize.
remainingGap: Graphify freshness was recorded as separate governance evidence.
why: Keep production KTO marker materialization under a mobile-safe cap.

how: Added E2E coverage for dropdown opening, screenshot success, and no tourism API call.
problem: The all filter should open without API calls, but production showed renderer freeze or screenshot timeout.
remainingGap: Initial KTO full load performance stayed in a separate hardening scope.
why: Prevent map floating capsule click freeze and Naver default control overlap.

how: Changed caps, batch size, requestAnimationFrame scheduling, and diff-based SDK mutation.
problem: Large marker insertion could still cause screenshot timeout and renderer freeze.
remainingGap: Payload slimming, edge cache, and server-side viewport query remain backend candidates.
why: Keep KTO map interactions responsive under the 300ms interaction target.

how: Made total required, kept KTO ON to one scope all call, kept filters local, and lazy-loaded detail by id.
problem: The list response total was optional and snapshot not-ready states were not fixed as retryable disabled KTO layer states.
remainingGap: Marker click detail sheet is not covered in E2E without a Naver client id, so lazy-load is covered through hook tests.
why: Align Web Front with the KTO scope all KV snapshot compact read model.
